title: macaranga motleyana subsp. griffithiana
intro: Macaranga motleyana subsp. griffithiana, also known as the tree of life or Malay meranti, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's leaves and bark are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that offer various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being dried and used in powders and capsules.
